## ⚠️ Breaking — read before updating

Earlier builds of this image wrote an `include_config_files:` directive into
`homeserver.yaml` to pull in `homeserver-overrides.yaml`. **Synapse has no such
directive** — it silently ignored the include, so the overrides (Postgres,
federation, TURN, registration, presence, retention) were never applied and
Synapse ran on a local **SQLite** database at `/data/homeserver.db` instead of
your configured Postgres.

This release fixes that. On first start after updating, Synapse will load the
overrides and switch to **Postgres** — which is empty unless you migrate. Your
existing rooms, users and history live in `/data/homeserver.db` and become
unreachable until you port them.

**Before relying on the update, choose one:**

- **Keep your data** — port SQLite → Postgres with
  [`synapse_port_db`](https://element-hq.github.io/synapse/latest/postgres.html#porting-from-sqlite),
  then start the updated container.
- **Start fresh on Postgres** — rename the old DB so it can't be picked up and
  let Synapse create a clean schema:
  `docker exec Matrix mv /data/homeserver.db /data/homeserver.db.old`

The init script now prints a loud warning at startup whenever a legacy
`/data/homeserver.db` is present, so you won't miss this.

## Fixes

- **Overrides are now actually loaded.** `homeserver-overrides.yaml` is passed to
  Synapse as a second `--config-path` in the synapse service (Synapse's
  `--config-path` is repeatable and merges in order — the documented pattern).
  The bogus `include_config_files:` block is no longer written, and the stale
  block is stripped from existing `homeserver.yaml` on upgrade.
  (Thanks to **@paulmeier** — issue #3 / PR #4.)
- **Repaired the release workflow** (`release.yml`) that was malformed and broke
  CI linting on `main`.

## Features

- **ASCII init-log banner.** The container now prints the Junker-der-Provinz
  banner at startup, matching the other repos in the family.

## Upgrade Notes

Existing installs: do the SQLite decision above **first**, then **Force Update**
in Unraid. `/data` is otherwise left untouched. Fresh installs need no action —
they go straight to Postgres as intended.
